original_hypothesis.title: FFactory initialization omits ADMIN_ROLE assignment, risking permanent inability to set router/tax params (dead-end ops state)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: FFactory.initialize() grants only DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE to msg.sender, but does not grant ADMIN_ROLE or CREATOR_ROLE. Critical functions setRouter() and setTaxParams() require onlyRole(ADMIN_ROLE), and createPair requires CREATOR_ROLE. If deployment scripts or post-deploy ops do not explicitly grant these roles, the factory can be stuck unable to configure router or create pairs, bricking the AMM subsystem.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a common operational deployment gap with real production impact. Validate role provisioning in deployment and upgrades; if missing, protocol may become non-functional or require redeploy.
deployment_assumptions: No post-deploy transaction grants ADMIN_ROLE and CREATOR_ROLE to intended operators
DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE holder does not execute role grants (or is renounced/lost)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment script for FFactory included that grants ADMIN_ROLE/CREATOR_ROLE; code only grants DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE in initialize() (contracts/fun/FFactory.sol).
preconditions: FFactory is deployed as upgradeable proxy or directly and initialize is called
Operators assume deployer automatically has ADMIN_ROLE/CREATOR_ROLE (but it does not)
No later grantRole calls are made

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Role-gated critical setters exist but roles are not assigned during init.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Deployment/ops must grant roles correctly; otherwise dead-end.

operational_questions: Where are ADMIN_ROLE and CREATOR_ROLE granted in production?
Is there a runbook ensuring role setup after initialization?
Is DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE retained by a multisig or renounced?
validation_requirements: Review on-chain role members for FFactory on target networks
Review deployment scripts for role-grant transactions (none provided here)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Not attacker-driven; manifests as an ops misconfiguration leading to outage.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: If attacker can influence deployment parameters (unlikely), encourage misconfigured deployment causing non-functional factory.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Cannot set router or create pairs; AMM subsystem unusable.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium—requires privileged role recovery or redeploy.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: ADMIN_ROLE has zero members; CREATOR_ROLE has zero members

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
